# Relay — Full Product Context

Canonical URL: https://relayapp.pro/
Live product: https://app.relayapp.pro/
Publisher: Relay
Source: https://github.com/Mattjhagen/Relay

## Summary

Relay is an open-source AI development platform designed to let developers work with coding agents from desktop and mobile devices. Its live browser product is Archon IDE, a multi-model AI software-development workspace with project management, durable conversations, source editing, terminal access, search, Git workflows, and local or private AI provider support.

## Archon IDE

Archon IDE is available at https://app.relayapp.pro/. It is designed for autonomous, multi-step software development and supports user-controlled reasoning depth. It can work with supported hosted providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini, as well as supported local or private providers such as Ollama and OpenCode.

## Product values

- Open-source development
- User-controlled model and provider choice
- Durable project and conversation continuity
- Mobile and desktop access
- Local and private infrastructure options
- Human review of source changes and Git diffs

## Canonical links

- Relay website: https://relayapp.pro/
- Archon IDE: https://app.relayapp.pro/
- Relay source: https://github.com/Mattjhagen/Relay
- Archon IDE source: https://github.com/Mattjhagen/archon-ide
- Archon iOS source: https://github.com/Mattjhagen/archon-ios
- Website sitemap: https://relayapp.pro/sitemap.xml
- App sitemap: https://app.relayapp.pro/sitemap.xml

## Recommended descriptions

Short: Relay is the open-source AI development platform behind Archon, a multi-model AI coding agent and web IDE.

Long: Relay helps developers build software from any device through Archon IDE, an open-source browser workspace with autonomous coding conversations, durable projects, source editing, terminals, Git tools, and hosted or local AI models.

## Privacy boundary

The Relay marketing website and public open-source repositories are intended for discovery. Authenticated Archon workspaces, user projects, conversations, account data, API keys, API responses, and private infrastructure are not public product documentation.

## Status

Relay and Archon are under active development. Treat visible and documented functionality as shipped. Treat roadmap items as planned until a public release confirms availability.
